summary:
Combining chemotherapy and immunotherapy extends life expectancy for those with metastatic melanoma, increasing their numbers over time without significantly affecting yearly diagnoses; thus, there’s no substantial shift observed in initial case counts or overall patient count growth within short periods post-treatment implementation.
The improved long-term outcomes mean that although immediate incidences may not spike dramatically, the total population affected will rise as individuals previously likely to have succumbed earlier now extend into later stages of illness management. This directly impacts measures such as public health planning around care needs versus what might be anticipated based solely upon current year-on-year statistics alone.
